title: Make `podman update` changes persistent
body:
Now that SQLite is the default store, we can persist changes to containers made with the `podman update` command. The actual logic is very simple, as that was never really what was holding us back; it was the BoltDB database not supporting it.

Also, make update emit events. Docker does it, we should as well.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Changes made by `podman update` are now persistent as long as the SQLite database backend is in use.
Updating containers with `podman update` now emits an event.
A Docker-compatible Update endpoint for Containers has been added to the remote API.
The `podman update` command now includes a new option, "--restart", to update the restart policy of existing containers.
```

comments.body: Tests are failing because we pull the OCI spec from disk for running containers, not from the DB, so it's not seeing the updates. We want to keep doing that because the spec on disk is much more complete. Maybe I can rewrite the spec on disk when we do an update? Will investigate.
Also, we apparently never implemented the Docker-compat REST endpoint for Update. I'll do that today.

What the hell. Getting `[+0874s]   Error: can only create exec sessions on running containers: container state improper` on trying to `podman exec` after the unpause. That works perfectly fine locally...
And update the PR description/release note as if I read this right it now also works with boltdb.
Also needs one more feature bit. `docker update` allows changing container restart policy; need to add that bit.
also your tests are not compiling
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 053890c9ba0537e935369e5f088b14963b94d6f2. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

The [failure of testPodmanRestartEnabledSystem()](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/137f209e-aafd-49da-8ec5-48ebb409660b/) here looks directly related, but it actually feels like an intended fix? On current main, creating a container and inspecting its default restart policy:
```
# podman run -d quay.io/libpod/busybox
# podman inspect -l --format '{{.HostConfig.RestartPolicy}}'
{ 0}
```
The output format is still the same when I update podman from 4.9.4-1.fc39 to 5.1.0~dev-1.20240412210338301579.pr22322.253.9115d1afb and query the existing container. But on a new one:
```
# podman inspect -l --format '{{.HostConfig.RestartPolicy}}'
{no 0}
```

So showing "no" instead of "" is indeed a more sensible default, but it's technically an API change. I sent https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/pull/1665 to adjust our tests, but please let me know if that wasn't actually intended.

Thanks!
Cockpit tests failed for commit c82facef2e0fba30c2240fe16a9ca6657854ae94. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Whoops, there was a second place that checked that, sorry! Fixed in https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/pull/1667
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Tests are green, this is finally ready.
